market: AI demand is real, but the market is shifting from rewarding raw capex ambition to rewarding deployable capacity, utilization, and cash-flow proof. Infrastructure and distribution remain the cleanest near-term winners.
tech: Reasoning and structured enterprise workflows are monetizing now. Broad autonomous agents in regulated environments are still ahead of the evidence.
risk: Top risks are capex outrunning monetization, power/interconnection delays constraining usable capacity, model-layer commoditization, and export-control tightening reshaping where AI can be built and sold.
